info.description: This problem set focuses on parasites and antiparasitic agents. The difficulty may vary a lot through out the set, since this set contains a mix of four different cognitive levels of complexity (recall, understand, apply, analyze). The questions are already reviewed, so mistakes should have already been minimized, but if you come across one, you can report through the Forms in the "Info and Links".

q1.prompt: A 50-year-old farmer presents with persistent bloody diarrhea and abdominal pain; stool microscopy reveals large, ovoid trophozoites covered in cilia. Which morphological feature is most diagnostic for the causative ciliate?

options.label: A terminal spine on the cyst wall

q2.prompt: A parasite commonly acquired through the consumption of raw apple snails is identified on microscopy as a third-stage larva measuring approximately 0.5 mm in length, with two chitinous rods at its anterior end. Which of the following conditions is most likely to result from infection with this organism?

q3.prompt: A 7-year-old child presents with a 4-month history of abdominal pain, straining during defecation, and persistent diarrhea containing mucus and streaks of blood. Physical examination reveals significant pallor and fatigue. A colonoscopy is performed, revealing numerous thin nematodes with long, whip-like anterior ends deeply embedded in the inflamed, hemorrhagic mucosa of the large intestine. Which of the following clinical manifestations is most likely to occur in this patient next?

q4.prompt: A 35-year-old patient with AIDS presents with chronic, voluminous watery diarrhea and significant weight loss. Stool examination reveals acid-fast oocysts, confirming a diagnosis of cryptosporidiosis. The physician explains that the clinical severity in this patient is exacerbated by an internal autoinfection cycle that increases the parasite burden entirely within the host's gastrointestinal tract. Which of the following mechanisms is directly responsible for this process of internal autoinfection?

options.label: Direct penetration of the colonic basement membrane by replicating trophozoites
Intracellular maturation of type II meronts into extra-epithelial macrogametes
Premature rupture of thick-walled oocysts within the gastric antrum
Spontaneous transformation of sporozoites into motile environmental spores
Rupture of thin-walled oocysts within the intestinal lumen

q5.prompt: During a routine stool examination of an asymptomatic food handler, spherical cysts measuring 14 µm in diameter are identified. The presence of four nuclei within these cysts is diagnostic for which organism?

q6.prompt: A 35-year-old man presents with fever, hemoptysis, and dyspnea after frequently consuming raw freshwater crabs. Which infective stage is responsible for this condition?

q7.prompt: A 36-year-old woman originally from Vietnam presents with recurrent right upper quadrant pain, fever, and jaundice. Imaging demonstrates biliary dilation with multiple stones and filing defects in the common and left hepatic ducts. A partial hepatectomy is performed, and microscopic examination of the biliary calculous material reveals collections of parasite eggs and a degenerated, fragmented nematode. Blood cultures concurrently grow mucoid, gram-negative bacilli. Which combination of pathogens is most likely associated with this clinical presentation?

q8.prompt: During a routine stool examination, a technician identifies a protozoan cyst with an eccentric karyosome, coarse peripheral chromatin, and 8 nuclei. Which of the following correctly identifies this organism?

q9.prompt: A patient in a region endemic for lymphatic filariasis presents with significant lymphedema of the scrotum and recurrent "filarial fever." What is the most common diagnostic method for the laboratory diagnosis of this condition?

options.label: Presence of rhabditiform larvae in the feces
Visualizing trophozoites in a skin biopsy
Identification of microfilariae in a thick blood film
Detection of adult worms in the stool
Microscopic examination of sputum for eggs

q10.prompt: A 32-year-old patient presents with a painful, migratory swelling on the abdominal wall following the consumption of raw freshwater fish. Clinical investigation confirms an infection with *Gnathostoma spinigerum*. Considering the specific host-parasite relationship established when this organism infects a human, which characteristic best describes the progression of the parasite in this patient?

options.label: The organism achieves sexual maturity and initiates egg production
The parasite undergoes metamorphosis and matures into an adult worm
The parasite undergoes asexual reproduction, increasing the total worm burden
The larvae migrate through tissues but fail to reach sexual maturity
The organism reaches its definitive habitat to complete its life cycle
